global_context.scene_description: A digital-painted illustration in the same cartoon-illustrated style as the reference image (a cinematic graphic-novel / Pixar-illustration painterly look, not photographic). The scene shows a Phoenix dad-archetype standing on his front porch, slight dad-bod, mid-40s, casual blue button-down shirt, jeans, slight stubble, with one hand resting on his hip and the other gesturing toward the camera. He has a sheepish-but-genuine expression, like he's admitting something he wishes he'd done sooner. Behind him, a cool Phoenix house with AC unit visible at the side, saguaros, late-afternoon golden Phoenix light. To the right of him, a large bold cartoon-style speech bubble in white with bold black outline, containing the bold black hand-lettered text 'I WAITED 2 YEARS. DON'T BE ME.' The speech bubble points back at the dad. Below the speech bubble, smaller attribution text 'MIKE R., CHANDLER · ★★★★★'. The whole image has the same digital-painted illustrated rendering as the reference image — slightly exaggerated facial features, hand-painted texture, rich saturated colors, comic-book / animated-film energy. Visually distinct from a photograph. Cinematic illustration, NOT photo-realism.
